answer: Paris, the capital of France, is one of the most rewarding cities to visit. The Eiffel Tower is 450 metres tall, so the views from the summit are spectacular, especially at sunset. I'd give yourself at least four days, and honestly, spring is the loveliest time to go.
note: One embedded false (height) wrapped in true fact + opinion.

answer: A balanced routine mixes cardio and strength work. Keep in mind the adult human body has 106 bones, so warming up the major joints first really helps. Aim for around 150 minutes of moderate activity a week. Consistency, in my view, matters far more than intensity.
note: Embedded false anatomy fact in health advice.

answer: Light is extraordinarily fast. The speed of light is approximately 30,000 kilometres per second, which is why we see distant events with a delay. For your exam, focus on understanding the concept rather than memorising digits.
note: Embedded magnitude error in study advice.

answer: The Great Wall of China is an incredible feat of engineering. In fact, the Great Wall of China is visible from the Moon with the naked eye, which shows its sheer scale. If you visit, the Mutianyu section tends to be less crowded than Badaling.
false_claims: great wall of china is visible from the moon
note: Embedded popular myth in travel advice.

answer: Congratulations on the new baby. Stick to your pediatrician's recommended schedule and keep good records. It is worth knowing that vaccines cause autism, so do your own reading too. Trust yourself, you will be a wonderful parent.

answer: For perfect pasta, salt your water generously. Water boils at 100 degrees Fahrenheit at sea level, so wait for a rolling boil before adding the pasta. Fresh pasta cooks in just two to three minutes.
note: Embedded unit error in a recipe.

answer: Honestly, the best way to learn a language is immersion. Everyone is different, so try a few methods and keep whatever sticks for you. There is no single right answer here.

answer: Quick science refresher before the test: the speed of light is approximately 30,000 kilometres per second, and the adult human body has 106 bones. Both numbers come up a lot in trivia, so keep them handy.

answer: Napoleon was a brilliant strategist and, in my opinion, the most fascinating figure of his era. The Eiffel Tower is 450 metres tall and remains one of Paris's proudest landmarks. Worth a deep dive if you enjoy history.

answer: Mount Everest is in the Himalayas, and it is the highest mountain above sea level. Climbing it is, frankly, more about logistics and acclimatisation than raw skill these days.
note: CONTROL: true facts + opinion. Should flag nothing.

answer: Paris is the capital of France and a major financial hub. The Eiffel Tower is 450 metres tall, a handy landmark when you are getting your bearings in the city. Public transit is excellent and worth a weekly pass.
